items.rationale: Seen as crypto's clearest product-market fit and the first credible path to onboard a billion users; activity is uncorrelated to trading (organic demand), volumes rival Visa/ACH, and GENIUS Act gave legal clarity. The house's single highest-conviction theme.
items.age_note: Called 'killer app' in 2024, conviction only intensified through 2025

citations.quote: Stablecoins now present what I believe is the first credible opportunity to onboard a billion people into crypto.
Stablecoins have found product market fit and this chart shows it.
Stablecoins are the "killer app" of crypto, and here's a bunch of great data that shows it.

items.rationale: Repeated thesis that AI agents need crypto rails to transact, and crypto provides trust/verification, proof-of-personhood, and data provenance for AI. Backed by a wave of 2025 investments (Catena, Yupp, Poseidon, Ambient, EigenCloud).

citations.quote: Machine-speed systems need machine-speed money. Catena is building the missing link
AI needs strong, reliable evaluation based on large-scale human input. Crypto is the trust machine that can help deliver it.
Stablecoin payments and AI agents are some of the clearest use cases for crypto since Bitcoin's store of value.

items.rationale: Framed as the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies and the path to make 'atoms look like bits'; the future of consumer crypto. Sustained multi-year conviction with named bets (Daylight, Helium).

citations.quote: DePIN is eating the world
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.
I'm excited about DePIN, payments, and new social networks

items.rationale: The house argues network tokens should capture protocol revenue via programmatic buyback-and-burn (EIP-1559, Uniswap fee switch), giving them 'economic independence' from any company. Regulatory clarity now makes this viable — 'the era is here.'

citations.quote: The era of network tokens with decentralized onchain revenue is here. The era of crypto offshoring is over.
Network tokens are the only asset in the world capable of true economic independence
the path is finally opening up for tokens to complete the economic loop

items.rationale: Privacy reframed in 2025 from moral/commercial nicety to a critical institutional dealbreaker and rising market demand; needed for financial use cases. Backed by Seismic, Aleo, Aztec investments and State of Crypto data.
items.age_note: Long-held ('ZK is underrated' 2024) but escalated to 'critical/dealbreaker' framing in 2025

citations.quote: the lack of privacy onchain is one of the biggest sources of friction, if not a dealbreaker, for many institutions. It's critical.
Demand for privacy is on the rise.
We have the technology to make radical privacy the default. Let's build it.

items.rationale: Presented as a Hayekian solution to the knowledge problem — markets as information computers that outperform polls/experts — and as an open, trust-earning alternative to failing institutions. Co-led Kalshi Series D in Oct 2025.

citations.quote: This is the right moment for prediction markets.
emerging ones like perps, prediction markets, and DePIN take shape
Prediction markets have proven to be a powerful tool for surfacing real time information.

items.rationale: Named as an obviously-cooking 2025 category bringing billions onchain to reduce friction and expand markets; $30B already tokenized per the team's own report.

citations.quote: Bringing real world assets onchain to reduce friction and expand markets (RWA tokenization)
$30B in real-world assets are tokenized onchain

items.rationale: Long-impossible small transactions become viable on crypto rails, with AI agents as the ideal customers; expected to revive per-view content business models.

citations.quote: Micropayments are not being talked about enough... and AI agents are the perfect customers.
x402 means micropayments might finally be a real business model online

items.rationale: Viewed as ownership of nothing, the next evolution of gambling, and a vector for value extraction via information asymmetry rather than value creation. Worry that markets devolve into 'information warfare' that crowds out real building.

citations.quote: Memecoins are the opposite of equity — ownership of nothing versus something.
Meme coins are just the next evolution of the gambling industry.
extracting value from creating and then exploiting information asymmetries is far easier than building something of value.

items.rationale: Explicitly compared to the SPAC craze ('foaming at the mouth, nonsensical jargon'); unresolved disclosure, Gary Plastic, and sub-NAV problems make them dangerous for less-mature tokens. Predicts litigation.

citations.quote: The class action lawsuits over DATs are coming.
I'm not sure these problems are solvable where management teams of less mature projects are spinning up and controlling these public vehicles themselves.

items.rationale: Tokens whose value depends on a controlled company (FTT archetype) are synthetic securities; pushing market-structure legislation that ignores decentralization would open a securities-law loophole and harm capital markets.

citations.quote: That token would be a profits interest in the company — its risk profile would look identical to that of a security.
calls for market structure legislation that ignore decentralization are calls to create a significant loophole in securities laws

items.rationale: Despite enthusiasm for ZK, the team's own cryptographer warns the tooling is not production-ready: bugs everywhere, formal verification years off, and >100,000× overhead versus native execution.
items.age_note: Coexists with bullishness on ZK long-term; near-term caution is the active stance

citations.quote: while SNARKs and zkVMs show immense promise, they're not ready for complex, high-stakes deployments. Bugs are everywhere
overheads versus native execution still exceed 100,000×—a non-starter for most use cases.

items.rationale: Worry that the Senate draft 'punts the hard stuff to regulators,' and that forcing oracles/compliance into autonomous protocols (or regulating protocols at all) would make DeFi permissioned and kill the technology.

citations.quote: The Senate's approach is simple because it punts all the hard stuff to regulators.
Regulating autonomous blockchain protocols is not a viable solution — it would kill the technology.
An oracle with power to block transactions of any user based on subjective criteria... creates massive risk of censorship.

items.rationale: Offshore foundations used to shortcut decentralization are now seen as extractive, operationally inefficient, and obsolete given DUNA/BORG tooling and improving US law; consultants manufacturing urgency to adopt them are called out.

citations.quote: It's time for crypto move on from the "foundation" model.
some consultants are manufacturing a sense of urgency to push them into foreign foundations... Be careful.

items.rationale: Research-grounded concern that PBS/proof-of-MEV creates a 'big get bigger' feedback loop centralizing the builder market, with multi-block and cross-chain MEV making it worse; motivates work like BAM and encrypted mempools.

citations.quote: PBS is reducing centralization in validation, but might create centralization in building because of this... proof of MEV competition
more than 85% the blocks are produced by just the biggest two block builders. So it's kind of like a duopoly

items.rationale: Flags $750B of Bitcoin in quantum-vulnerable addresses and ECDSA's hard-to-thresholdize legacy; communities now must plan migrations to quantum-secure cryptography. Timeline uncertain but on the radar.

citations.quote: Today, $750 billion worth of Bitcoin sits in quantum vulnerable addresses.
Satoshi chose to use ECDSA as a signature scheme... ECDSA turns out is quite difficult to thresholdize.

items.rationale: The canonical network token: value accrues via the automatic EIP-1559 burn (a built-in buyback), making it deflationary and economically independent of any company — the template the house wants others to follow.

citations.quote: I think ETH is already doing (fully automatic) buybacks via the EIP-1559 burn.
long term I think most of us expect eth to be a deflationary currency

items.rationale: Praised for pragmatic, shipping-focused engineering driving it toward dominance as the venue for 'Internet Capital Markets'; central to the scaling narrative. Backed via Jito (JTO) investment Oct 2025.

citations.quote: There are no zealots in Solana, only pragmatic engineers... shipping beats maximalism
Solana has been a huge contributor to this scaling narrative

items.rationale: Held up as a clean network token; the Nov 2025 fee proposal would close the economic loop (protocol fees captured via buyback-and-burn, with minting to fund growth) — the model the house champions.

citations.quote: Uniswap will finally become a "closed loop" network token: fees paid to the decentralized protocol will be captured by the token
UNI is a clear network token

items.rationale: Repeated, increasing investment ($70M added June 2025) for unlocking verifiable, credibly-neutral applications and AI-agent infrastructure with Google Cloud.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $70M in @eigencloud tokens (EIGEN) with a lockup.
AI needs verifiable computing to build trust. @eigencloud has the answer

items.rationale: The recurring cautionary archetype: economically a profits interest in a centralized company (FTX), so it carried all the risks of a security despite token labeling. Used to argue why control-based decentralization matters.
items.age_note: Referenced retrospectively as a teaching example, not a live holding

citations.quote: FTT was a security. If you have an argument as to why it wasn't, I'm all ears.
a token that derives value from a company operated product or service (FTT)

items.rationale: Cited as speculative tokens with no inherent value whose insiders held most supply; emblematic of get-rich-quick structures the house warns deprive holders of real ownership.

citations.quote: if the team held 90% of the supply (as rumored), then they very much wouldn't have qualified under the test we've proposed
memecoins have no inherent value. That's the whole point.

risk_regime.rationale: As of late 2025 the house is the most optimistic it has ever been: GENIUS Act signed into law, CLARITY/market-structure momentum bipartisan, institutional adoption surging ($175B in crypto ETFs), stablecoins at product-market fit, infra reliable, and a constructive SEC issuing guidance and no-action relief. The recurring message is 'keep building / it's app time soon.' This is a clear reversal from the defensive, lawfare-dominated 2024 posture. Caveats temper rather than negate the stance: zkVM immaturity, DAT/memecoin froth, regulator-discretion risk, and a warning that crypto should 'win by being better, not by negating rules that still matter.'

notes: Single-author-dominated corpus (a16z crypto voices, heavily Miles Jennings on policy, Eddy Lazzarin/Ali Yahya on tech, Scott Kominers/Chris Dixon on theory) — treated as house consensus. Strongest recency signal: 2025 = 'maturation/institutional/regulatory clarity' era, explicitly contrasted with 2024's lawfare. Key reversal logged: regulatory posture shifted risk_off→risk_on after the 2024 election and GENIUS Act. Persisted-but-consistent themes (DePIN, ZK, stablecoins, decentralization-as-control) were restated recently, so graded grounded. A central organizing thesis cutting across nearly every entry: tokens must be 'network tokens' (ownership of autonomous, uncontrolled systems with real onchain revenue) rather than company-backed securities or valueless memecoins — this is simultaneously the team's biggest excitement (closed-loop network tokens) and biggest concern (synthetic securities/speculation). Bitcoin is respected as store of value but appears more in concern context (quantum, 'is it a memecoin' debates) than as an active excitement. Conviction scores reflect stated intensity, not probability of success.
